---
title: "Full resoultion in VMWare"
date: 2007-03-10
forum: Absolute Beginner Talk
---

### Post by flightrecorder on 2007-03-10
I just installed ubuntu running in vmware, everything is running fine but the max resolution is 800x600. My max in windows is 1600x1200. How do I fix this? Does it have something to do with drivers in linux or something with VMware? 
Thanks.

---

### Post by bernied on 2007-03-10
I think you just need to install vmware-tools in the VM. It provides a video driver, amongst other things.

---

### Post by flightrecorder on 2007-03-10
where can i get vmware tools? I tried to get vmware server (which i think had tools listed in it), but now i need a serial number for that.

---

### Post by towsonu2003 on 2007-03-10
have a look at this file, it might help (it's my xorg.conf in vmware-installed ubuntu fesity dvelopment branch; I think you can use it in a guest host):
```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"IMPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

#Section "InputDevice"
#	Driver		"wacom"
#	Identifier	"stylus"
#	Option		"Device"	"/dev/wacom"	# Change to 
#							# /dev/input/event
#							# for USB
#	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
#	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection

#Section "InputDevice"
#	Driver		"wacom"
#	Identifier	"eraser"
#	Option		"Device"	"/dev/wacom"	# Change to 
#							# /dev/input/event
#							# for USB
#	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
#	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection

#Section "InputDevice"
#	Driver		"wacom"
#	Identifier	"cursor"
#	Option		"Device"	"/dev/wacom"	# Change to 
#							# /dev/input/event
#							# for USB
#	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
#	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier  "VMware SVGA"
    Driver      "vmware"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier      "vmware"
    VendorName      "VMware, Inc"
    HorizSync 1-10000
    VertRefresh 1-10000

    ModeLine "640x480" 100 640 700 800 900 480 500 600 700
    ModeLine "1280x480" 100 1280 1300 1400 1500 480 500 600 700
    ModeLine "640x960" 100 640 700 800 900 960 1000 1100 1200
    ModeLine "800x600" 100 800 900 1000 1100 600 700 800 900
    ModeLine "1600x600" 100 1600 1700 1800 1900 600 700 800 900
    ModeLine "800x1200" 100 800 900 1000 1100 1200 1300 1400 1500
    ModeLine "1024x768" 100 1024 1100 1200 1300 768 800 900 1000
    ModeLine "2048x768" 100 2048 2100 2200 2300 768 800 900 1000
    ModeLine "1024x1536" 100 1024 1100 1200 1300 1536 1600 1700 1800
    ModeLine "1152x864" 100 1152 1200 1300 1400 864 900 1000 1100
    ModeLine "2304x864" 100 2304 2400 2500 2600 864 900 1000 1100
    ModeLine "1152x1728" 100 1152 1200 1300 1400 1728 1800 1900 2000
    ModeLine "1280x1024" 100 1280 1300 1400 1500 1024 1100 1200 1300
    ModeLine "2560x1024" 100 2560 2600 2700 2800 1024 1100 1200 1300
    ModeLine "1280x2048" 100 1280 1300 1400 1500 2048 2100 2200 2300
    ModeLine "1600x1200" 100 1600 1700 1800 1900 1200 1300 1400 1500
    ModeLine "3200x1200" 100 3200 3300 3400 3500 1200 1300 1400 1500

EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
    Device      "VMware SVGA"
    Monitor     "vmware"
    # Don't specify DefaultColorDepth unless you know what you're
    # doing. It will override the driver's preferences which can
    # cause the X server not to run if the host doesn't support the
    # depth.
    Subsection "Display"
        # VGA mode: better left untouched
        Depth       4
        Modes       "640x480"
        ViewPort    0 0
    EndSubsection
    Subsection "Display"
        Depth       8
        Modes       "1280x800"
        ViewPort    0 0
    EndSubsection
    Subsection "Display"
        Depth       15
        Modes       "1280x800"
        ViewPort    0 0
    EndSubsection
    Subsection "Display"
        Depth       16
        Modes       "1280x800"
        ViewPort    0 0
    EndSubsection
    Subsection "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes       "1200x700"
        ViewPort    0 0
    EndSubsection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```
I'm using 1200x700 resolution

### Post by VorDesigns on 2007-03-10
> **flightrecorder said:**
> i'm not getting anywhere. :) why is this harder then it should be?
You stated that you are using VMWare Player and I'm guessing you downloaded a version of Ubuntu 6.0.1 VMSystem available at VMWare.  I'm guessing tools wern't installed.
Try this one. [http://www.vmware.com/vmtn/appliances/directory/484]("http://www.vmware.com/vmtn/appliances/directory/484"), it states VMWare tools are installed.
If you did download that version, you may have the same problem I have where it just won't seem to work.

### Post by tagra123 on 2007-03-10
If you've already download the server tar file you can open it and look for the -- I think its called windows.iso. Anyway just find the right iso file and extract it.

Once extracted you can mount it or tell vmplayer to use it instead of the cd by editing the vmx file. 

#These lines if using the hardware.
#ide1:0.present = "TRUE"
#ide1:0.fileName = "auto detect"
#ide1:0.deviceType = "cdrom-raw"
#ide1:0.autodetect = "TRUE"

#This line boots from iso image
#ide1:0.present = "TRUE"
#ide1:0.fileName = "/media/tempdrive/mpgfiles/someisofile.iso"
#ide1:0.deviceType = "cdrom-image"


Be sure to remove the comments  from hardware if using hardware or from iso image if using iso images and add the correct filename

---

### Post by Jose Catre-Vandis on 2007-03-10
> **tagra123 said:**
> If you've already download the server tar file you can open it and look for the -- I think its called windows.iso. Anyway just find the right iso file and extract it.

Once extracted you can mount it or tell vmplayer to use it instead of the cd by editing the vmx file. 

#These lines if using the hardware.
#ide1:0.present = "TRUE"
#ide1:0.fileName = "auto detect"
#ide1:0.deviceType = "cdrom-raw"
#ide1:0.autodetect = "TRUE"

#This line boots from iso image
#ide1:0.present = "TRUE"
#ide1:0.fileName = "/media/tempdrive/mpgfiles/someisofile.iso"
#ide1:0.deviceType = "cdrom-image"


Be sure to remove the comments  from hardware if using hardware or from iso image if using iso images and add the correct filename

It's called linux.iso. It is recommended to install inside the VM rather than from the Vmware server menu

### Post by tagra123 on 2007-03-10
linux.iso is for linux guest systems...  There is one for window guest systems.

its in the lib/isoimages of the archive

there is 

linux.iso
solaris.iso

and windows.iso  (the file you want if running windows as the guest in the vmplayer)

---

### Post by AndyCooll on 2007-03-10
> **flightrecorder said:**
> i'm using server.

I tried these instructions ([https://help.ubuntu.com/community/VMware/Tools](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/VMware/Tools)) but no dice. it seemed to install tools correctly but i still cant change the resolution.

In which case, I believe you need to login to Ubuntu. Then on the VMware toolbar there should be "VM" and under that drop down menu "Install VMware Tools"

:cool:

---

### Post by scxtt on 2007-03-10
anytime i see someone w/ VM resolution problems, i just tell them to add "HorizSync" and "VertRefresh" lines (for their monitor) to /etc/X11/xorg.conf of the linux guest ... always works for me ...

---

### Post by tagra123 on 2007-03-10
One other thing to check.

gedit  ~/.vmware/preferences

check for these settings -- add them if they are not there and see if it fixes it for you.

pref.autoFitFullScreen = "fitHostToGuest"
pref.autoFitGuestToWindow = "TRUE"
pref.autoFit = "TRUE"

---
